text: Joseph was taken to Egypt and bought from the Ishmaelites by Potiphar, an Egyptian official.
The LORD was with Joseph, and he prospered in his Egyptian master's household.
Potiphar saw the LORD was with him and gave success to everything he did.
Joseph found favor and became Potiphar's personal attendant, in charge of his household and possessions.
From that time the LORD blessed Potiphar's house for Joseph's sake, in field and household alike.
Potiphar left everything in Joseph's care, concerning himself only with his food; Joseph was well-built and handsome.
After a while Potiphar's wife noticed Joseph and said, come to bed with me.
He refused, saying his master trusted him with everything and withheld nothing but her.
How could he do such wickedness and sin against God?
Though she spoke to him day after day, he refused to sleep with her or even be near her.
One day he went into the house to work, and none of the household servants were there.
She grabbed his cloak, saying come to bed with me; he fled outside, leaving the cloak in her hand.
When she saw he had fled leaving his cloak,
she called her servants and said, look, my husband brought a Hebrew to make sport of us; he tried to violate me and I screamed.
When he heard me scream, he left his cloak and ran outside.
She kept the cloak until her husband came home.
She told him the same story, that the Hebrew slave came to make sport of her.
When I screamed, he left his cloak and ran outside.
When his master heard his wife's account, he burned with anger.
Joseph's master put him in prison, where the king's prisoners were held.
But the LORD was with Joseph, showed him kindness, and granted him favor with the prison warden.
The warden put Joseph in charge of all the prisoners and everything done there.
The warden paid no attention to anything under Joseph's care, because the LORD was with him and gave him success.
